name: Firefox
description: Free, open-source web browser developed by the Mozilla Foundation and its subsidiary Mozilla Corporation.

years.notes: Phoenix era (Sept 2002 – Apr 2003) — stylised red bird, used before the Firebird/Firefox renames. Recreated SVG on Wikimedia Commons from the original Mozilla Phoenix credit page.
Original Firefox fox-and-globe by Jon Hicks (concept by Daniel Burka, sketched by Stephen Desroches). Shipped with Firefox 1.0 in November 2004 and used through Firefox 3.0 (2009).
Flat redesign by Sean Martell, shipped with Firefox 23 (August 2013) — simpler gradients, refined globe and fox silhouette. Used through Firefox 56.
Photon redesign shipped with Firefox 57 (Quantum, November 2017) — flatter fox, textureless globe, brighter palette. Used through Firefox 69.
Family-of-icons era introduced with Firefox 70 (October 2019) — purple globe, simplified fox without visible legs. Current Firefox browser mark (unchanged through 2026).

notes: Skipped millésimes: 2003 Firebird (no authoritative vector source on Wikimedia/archive — only low-res raster references), 2005 Firefox 1.5/2.0 (Wikimedia's Mozilla_Firefox_logo_2004.svg explicitly covers 2004–2009, so the 2005 refinement is not separately vectorised), 2009 Firefox 3.5 (only a 512×512 raster PNG exists on Wikimedia — no project raster-to-SVG tool available yet), 2024 (Firefox itself did not rebrand; the 2024 Mozilla rebrand applies to the parent company wordmark, not the Firefox browser mark).
